content.Text: [Tue 2026-04-21 03:15 PDT] Create a new interactive Graph Link Predictor tool at docs/link-predictor.html for this GraphVisual project. This tool predicts missing/future edges in a graph using multiple link prediction algorithms. ## Requirements 1. **Self-contained HTML file** using the project's `styles.css` (linked as `href="styles.css"`). Include the same sidebar navigation as other tools (copy from docs/anomaly.html pattern with all the nav links). Add a new entry for this tool: `🔮Link Predictor` in the Tools section. 2. **Layout**: Left control panel (300px) + right canvas area, matching the project's dark theme style. Use CSS variables like `var(--surface)`, `var(--border)`, `var(--accent)`, `var(--text)`, `var(--bg)` etc. 3. **Graph Input**: - Canvas-based interactive graph editor where users can click to add nodes, click+drag between nodes to add edges - Text input for adjacency list (format: "A-B, B-C, C-D") - 8 preset graphs: Social Network, Citation Network, Protein Interaction, Airline Routes, Collaboration, Internet Topology, Food Web, Karate Club 4. **4 Link Prediction Algorithms**: - **Common Neighbors**: score(u,v) = |N(u) ∩ N(v)| - **Jaccard Coefficient**: score(u,v) = |N(u) ∩ N(v)| / |N(u) ∪ N(v)| - **Adamic-Adar Index**: score(u,v) = Σ(1/log|N(w)|) for w in N(u) ∩ N(v) - **Preferential Attachment**: score(u,v) = |N(u)| × |N(v)| 5. **Visualization**: - Force-directed layout on Canvas for the graph - Existing edges shown solid, predicted edges shown as dashed lines with color intensity based on score - Top-K slider (5-50) to control how many predictions to show - Hover on predicted edges shows score tooltip 6. **Autonomous Analysis** (agentic feature): - "Auto-Analyze" button that runs all 4 algorithms, finds consensus predictions (edges predicted by multiple algorithms), ranks by combined score - Proactive insights panel: "Most likely missing link", "Highest-connected hub likely to gain edges", "Isolated clusters that may bridge" - Recommendations like "Node X is structurally similar to Node Y - connection likely" 7. **Results Table**: Sortable table showing predicted edges, per-algorithm scores, consensus rank. Export as JSON/CSV. 8. **Stats bar**: Show node count, edge count, density, predictions found, consensus predictions count. Make all JavaScript inline in the HTML file. The tool should work standalone in a browser. Use Canvas API for graph visualization with pan/zoom support. Make it look professional and match the dark theme of the project. Also update docs/index.html to add the Link Predictor to the sidebar navigation and to any tool listing on the overview page. Add it under the Tools section with icon 🔮 and label "Link Predictor".
